draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: AI workspace users prefer metric-focused compact displays over narrative explanations
facade.label: Numbers in cards
facade.acceptImplies: user values quick-scan dashboards, KPI cards, and numerical summaries for workspace analytics
facade.rejectImplies: user wants contextual storytelling, explanations, and relational narrative over isolated data points

facade.hypothesis: An ai workspace benefits from layered, floating UI elements that appear and dismiss with smooth fade and scale transitions rather than hard cuts
facade.axisTargeted: Motion vocabulary: fade/scale transitions vs instant appearance
facade.label: Floating panels
facade.acceptImplies: Explore soft entrance animations, staggered layer reveals, and dismissal via shrink-fade patterns; prioritize perceived depth through motion
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward instant UI appearance, no entrance animations, and flat layering without motion-based depth cues

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces may prefer calming, non-aggressive color schemes that feel inviting rather than technical/harsh
facade.label: Soft pastel palette
facade.acceptImplies: Builder values approachable, human-centered aesthetics; may gravitate toward cream, sage, soft blue tones
facade.rejectImplies: Builder prefers bold contrast, cooler/neutral grays, or darker technical palettes; sees softness as unprofessional or distracting

evidence.hypothesis: AI workspace users prefer metric-focused compact displays over narrative explanations
evidence.implication: user values quick-scan dashboards, KPI cards, and numerical summaries for workspace analytics

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer low-contrast, distraction-free interfaces with ample breathing room for an AI workspace
facade.label: Dark minimal workspace
facade.acceptImplies: User favors dark mode and sparse layouts; probe next: accent color warmth or cool tones
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer brighter, more populated interfaces; probe next: layered depth or card-based organization

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users of AI workspaces prefer keeping tools visible in a persistent sidebar while content expands in a central area, with secondary actions in floating/modal panels rather than nested menus
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation topology — persistent left column vs collapsible vs floating
facade.label: Sidebar with floating panel
facade.acceptImplies: Explore density within sidebar (icon-only, labels, sections) and relationship between sidebar state and main content width
facade.rejectImplies: Test top navigation, full-screen context switching, or tab-based organization instead

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace value approachable, human-centered voice over corporate formality
facade.label: Casual and warm
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into conversational tone, lighter visual language, emoji/playful elements, less jargon in UI copy
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward polished, credential-forward, serious tone—position as enterprise-grade tool requiring professional respect

facade.hypothesis: AI workspace users prefer high-contrast dark UI elements for navigation and tool panels, reducing visual fatigue during long work sessions
facade.axisTargeted: Color and contrast strategy — bright/colorful vs dark/minimal
facade.label: Dark sidebar
facade.acceptImplies: Build with dark mode as primary, use contrast hierarchies to guide attention to active tools and metrics
facade.rejectImplies: Explore lighter, more neutral backgrounds with softer contrast; dark UI may feel too technical or draining for this use case

draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, prompts, and outputs with a clean, productivity-focused design.
